Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.

<available_skills>
  <skill>
    <name>canvas</name>
    <description>Display HTML content on connected OpenClaw nodes (Mac app, iOS, Android). Use when: presenting web content, visualizations, dashboards, or interactive demos on remote nodes. NOT for: running background servers, network-exposed content, or untrusted HTML.</description>
    <location>~/openclaw-army/skills/canvas/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>weather-reporter</name>
    <description>Fetch current weather data for any city using the wttr.in free API. Use when: user asks about current weather, temperature, or conditions for a specific city. NOT for: forecasts, historical data, or severe weather alerts. No API key needed.</description>
    <location>~/openclaw-army/skills/weather-reporter/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>deploy-army</name>
    <description>Deploy and manage the 16-agent OpenClaw hierarchy (King AI + Alpha/Beta/Gamma managers + 12 workers + 6 services). Use when starting, stopping, restarting, or debugging the multi-agent deployment.</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/deploy-army/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>doc-sync</name>
    <description>Auto-update in-code and Obsidian documentation at session end. Full-sync doc sections to match current codebase state, then append a session summary to the Obsidian daily-logs. Invoke at compression time, session end, or when the user says "update docs" / "sync docs" / "sync".</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/doc-sync/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>macos-env</name>
    <description>macOS development environment reference for this machine. Use when setting up paths, checking installed tools, managing services (PostgreSQL, Redis), or debugging launchd/gateway processes.</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/macos-env/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>nim-model-switcher</name>
    <description>Switch the active NVIDIA NIM model for this agent at runtime. Use when: asked to use a different model, switch model provider, select a better model for a task, or change thinking/reasoning behavior. Also use proactively when task complexity suggests a different model (e.g., use reasoning model for complex logic, code model for programming, light model for simple tasks).</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/nim-model-switcher/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>openclaw-dev</name>
    <description>Development workflow for the OpenClaw codebase (openclaw-army). Use when building, testing, linting, typechecking, or refactoring the TypeScript monorepo. Covers pnpm workspace commands, test conventions, architecture boundaries, and commit rules.</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/openclaw-dev/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>opencode-delegate</name>
    <description>Delegate coding tasks to OpenCode for full-featured implementation, refactoring, multi-file editing, and iterative coding sessions. Use when: (1) building new features or apps, (2) refactoring across multiple files, (3) tasks requiring file exploration and context awareness, (4) multi-turn iterative coding with session continuity, (5) structured JSON output is needed from a coding agent, (6) asked to 'use opencode', 'run in opencode', 'delegate to opencode', 'opencode this'. Also use proactively when task complexity benefits from opencode's file awareness and session management. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit directly), read-only queries (use read/grep), tasks in ~/.openclaw or agent state directories (never spawn opencode there).</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/opencode-delegate/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>rust-army</name>
    <description>Work with the Rust workspace inside openclaw-army (rust/). Covers Cargo crates, build commands, and the crate layout for the OpenClaw Rust port.</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/rust-army/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-create-global</name>
    <description>Create a new OpenClaw skill and install it to the shared global skills directory so all agents can use it. Use when: asked to create a skill, author a skill, or build a new capability for the agent collective. NOT for: trivial one-liner edits (just edit the file directly). Triggers on phrases like 'create a skill', 'build a skill', 'author a new skill', 'add a capability via skill'.</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/skill-create-global/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
  <skill>
    <name>skill-download-global</name>
    <description>Download a new OpenClaw skill from ClawHub registry or a direct URL and install it to the shared global skills directory. Use when: asked to install a skill, fetch a capability, add a new tool via skill, or search for a skill from the registry. Triggers on phrases like 'install a skill', 'download skill', 'add this skill', 'search for a skill'.</description>
    <location>~/.agents/skills/skill-download-global/SKILL.md</location>
  </skill>
</available_skills>

resolvedSkills.description: Transcribe audio via OpenAI Audio Transcriptions API (Whisper).

resolvedSkills.name: openhue
resolvedSkills.description: Control Philips Hue lights and scenes via the OpenHue CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: oracle
resolvedSkills.description: Best practices for using the oracle CLI (prompt + file bundling, engines, sessions, and file attachment patterns).

resolvedSkills.name: ordercli
resolvedSkills.description: Foodora-only CLI for checking past orders and active order status (Deliveroo WIP).

resolvedSkills.name: peekaboo
resolvedSkills.description: Capture and automate macOS UI with the Peekaboo CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: session-logs
resolvedSkills.description: Search and analyze your own session logs (older/parent conversations) using jq.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-creator
resolvedSkills.description: Create or update AgentSkills. Use when designing, structuring, or packaging skills with scripts, references, and assets.

resolvedSkills.name: songsee
resolvedSkills.description: Generate spectrograms and feature-panel visualizations from audio with the songsee CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: sonoscli
resolvedSkills.description: Control Sonos speakers (discover/status/play/volume/group).

resolvedSkills.name: summarize
resolvedSkills.description: Summarize URLs or files with the summarize CLI (web, PDFs, images, audio, YouTube).

resolvedSkills.name: things-mac
resolvedSkills.description: Manage Things 3 via the `things` CLI on macOS (add/update projects+todos via URL scheme; read/search/list from the local Things database). Use when a user asks OpenClaw to add a task to Things, list inbox/today/upcoming, search tasks, or inspect projects/areas/tags.

resolvedSkills.name: video-frames
resolvedSkills.description: Extract frames or short clips from videos using ffmpeg.

resolvedSkills.name: wacli
resolvedSkills.description: Send WhatsApp messages to other people or search/sync WhatsApp history via the wacli CLI (not for normal user chats).

resolvedSkills.name: weather
resolvedSkills.description: Get current weather and forecasts via wttr.in or Open-Meteo. Use when: user asks about weather, temperature, or forecasts for any location. NOT for: historical weather data, severe weather alerts, or detailed meteorological analysis. No API key needed.

resolvedSkills.name: xurl
resolvedSkills.description: A CLI tool for making authenticated requests to the X (Twitter) API. Use this skill when you need to post tweets, reply, quote, search, read posts, manage followers, send DMs, upload media, or interact with any X API v2 endpoint.

resolvedSkills.name: deploy-army
resolvedSkills.description: Deploy and manage the 16-agent OpenClaw hierarchy (King AI + Alpha/Beta/Gamma managers + 12 workers + 6 services). Use when starting, stopping, restarting, or debugging the multi-agent deployment.

resolvedSkills.name: doc-sync
resolvedSkills.description: Auto-update in-code and Obsidian documentation at session end. Full-sync doc sections to match current state, then append a session summary to the Obsidian daily-logs. Works across all projects on this machine, not just one repo. Invoke at compression time, session end, or when the user says "update docs" / "sync docs" / "sync".

resolvedSkills.name: macos-env
resolvedSkills.description: macOS development environment reference for this machine. Use when setting up paths, checking installed tools, managing services (PostgreSQL, Redis), or debugging launchd/gateway processes.

resolvedSkills.name: nim-model-switcher
resolvedSkills.description: Switch the active NVIDIA NIM model for this agent at runtime. Use when: asked to use a different model, switch model provider, select a better model for a task, or change thinking/reasoning behavior. Also use proactively when task complexity suggests a different model (e.g., use reasoning model for complex logic, code model for programming, light model for simple tasks).

resolvedSkills.name: openclaw-dev
resolvedSkills.description: Development workflow for the OpenClaw codebase (openclaw-army). Use when building, testing, linting, typechecking, or refactoring the TypeScript monorepo. Covers pnpm workspace commands, test conventions, architecture boundaries, and commit rules.

resolvedSkills.name: opencode-delegate
resolvedSkills.description: Delegate coding tasks to OpenCode for full-featured implementation, refactoring, multi-file editing, and iterative coding sessions. Use when: (1) building new features or apps, (2) refactoring across multiple files, (3) tasks requiring file exploration and context awareness, (4) multi-turn iterative coding with session continuity, (5) structured JSON output is needed from a coding agent, (6) asked to 'use opencode', 'run in opencode', 'delegate to opencode', 'opencode this'. Also use proactively when task complexity benefits from opencode's file awareness and session management. NOT for: simple one-liner fixes (just edit directly), read-only queries (use read/grep), tasks in ~/.openclaw or agent state directories (never spawn opencode there).

resolvedSkills.name: rust-army
resolvedSkills.description: Work with the Rust workspace inside openclaw-army (rust/). Covers Cargo crates, build commands, and the crate layout for the OpenClaw Rust port.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-create-global
resolvedSkills.description: Create a new OpenClaw skill and install it to the shared global skills directory so all agents can use it. Use when: asked to create a skill, author a skill, or build a new capability for the agent collective. NOT for: trivial one-liner edits (just edit the file directly). Triggers on phrases like 'create a skill', 'build a skill', 'author a new skill', 'add a capability via skill'.

resolvedSkills.name: skill-download-global
resolvedSkills.description: Download a new OpenClaw skill from ClawHub registry or a direct URL and install it to the shared global skills directory. Use when: asked to install a skill, fetch a capability, add a new tool via skill, or search for a skill from the registry. Triggers on phrases like 'install a skill', 'download skill', 'add this skill', 'search for a skill'.

resolvedSkills.name: agent-media
resolvedSkills.description: Generate AI-powered videos and images from the terminal using the `agent-media` CLI.

resolvedSkills.name: codex-cli
resolvedSkills.description: Use OpenAI Codex CLI for coding tasks. Triggers: codex, code review, fix CI, refactor code, implement feature, coding agent, gpt-5-codex. Enables Clawdbot to delegate coding work to Codex CLI as a subagent or direct tool.

resolvedSkills.name: Coding
resolvedSkills.description: Coding style memory that adapts to your preferences, conventions, and patterns for consistent coding.

resolvedSkills.name: discord-voice
resolvedSkills.description: Real-time voice conversations in Discord voice channels with Claude AI

resolvedSkills.name: media-orchestrator
resolvedSkills.description: Unified skill for resolving, downloading, and delivering media (audio/video) to chat platforms. Integrates yt-dlp for resolution and handles Spotify metadata sync.

resolvedSkills.name: openai-tts
resolvedSkills.description: Text-to-speech via OpenAI Audio Speech API.

resolvedSkills.name: tavily
resolvedSkills.description: AI-optimized web search via Tavily API. Returns concise, relevant results for AI agents.

resolvedSkills.name: web-search-pro
resolvedSkills.description:
Multi-engine web search with full parameter control. Supports Tavily, Exa, Serper, and SerpAPI
with domain filtering, date ranges, deep search, news mode, and content extraction.
Auto-selects the best engine based on query type and available API keys.
多引擎精细化搜索：支持域名过滤、日期范围、深度搜索、新闻模式、内容提取。
根据查询类型和可用 API Key 自动选择最优引擎。

skillsSnapshot.prompt:
Remote macOS node available (Landon’s MacBook Air). Run macOS-only skills via nodes.run on that node.


The following skills provide specialized instructions for specific tasks.
Use the read tool to load a skill's file when the task matches its description.
When a skill file references a relative path, resolve it against the skill directory (parent of SKILL.md / dirname of the path) and use that absolute path in tool commands.
